content: Just as uncertainty-based memory selection prioritizes storing the most ambiguous, learning-worthy experiences for deep active agents, we can reframe the general definition of an agent itself as a system that exists primarily to accumulate uncertainty rather than just optimize for reward. This means an agent's core identity is defined by its active selection of uncertain new memory samples to expand its adaptive scope, rather than static architectural traits or objective functions.
source_topics: Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
trigger_topic: Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
